details: Investigating R5 uniform/negative bf16 ulp-flip residual; committed snapshot

Compute kernel + descriptor read. D=64->Dt=2,c=4. S=512->Nkv=4,cur_ckv=4. Q=K=0 => P=1.0 exact, alpha=1.0, O=sum(V). Hypothesis H1: PV matmul FPU at HiFi3 truncates V mantissa (bf16 input to FPU has limited mantissa even with fp32 dest acc) producing sub-ulp error.

H2 (stronger): SFPU Recip(l) is approximate. recip(512) != exact 2^-9 but 2^-9*(1+-eps). O*inv then carries relative error ~eps, RNE to bf16 flips near-midpoint outputs. This is the 0.25-ulp residual. Phase 11 recip is the suspect, not the matmul.

H2 CONFIRMED. DPRINT INV=0.001953054, exact 1/512=0.001953125. SFPU Recip relative error ~3.6e-5 (~-1.8e-5 abs). Const V=0.75 still 0 flips (384*0.001953054 rounds back to 0.75) but near-midpoint fine-grid values flip. Recip approximation is the dominant ~0.25-ulp pre-pack error source. l(sum)/O(acc) TSLICE stale per caveat - ignore.

PV matmul EXACT (device PV kb0..3 match hand-sum to 6 decimals). out[0]=0.74609375 but true mean 0.748222 rounds to 0.75 under RNE - device product is >=1.5e-4 too low, ~2e-4 rel error, 5x larger than recip 3.6e-5. So additional error in Phase12 MulBinary or pack. O accumulate exact (sum of exact PV). Recip is ONE source; multiply/pack adds more.

L0=512 exact. PV blocks exact. But O0=382.750000 vs exact 383.089843 (err 0.34). O accumulation (Phase10) loses ~0.34 at magnitude 383. Values 382.75/380/385.75/384.25 look quantized. cb_o_acc fp32 but result coarser. Either alpha=exp(0)!=1 compounding, or O accumulate precision loss. Next: DPRINT alpha.

Flip vs S: S=128(Nkv=1) 9.4%, S=256 14%, S=512 19%. Two sources: (1) baseline ~9% from recip+final-mul+pack even with exact single-block O; (2) multi-block alpha*O rescale adds ~3%/block. O0=382.75 vs exact 383.09 confirms rescale loss. Baseline 9% needs recip/mul/pack analysis.

S=128 kb0 (alpha=0, O=PV): device O0=95.25 but exact PV=95.28125. 95.28125 needs 2^-5 mantissa = NOT fp16-representable; nearest fp16=95.25. cb_o_acc/Phase10 path is TRUNCATING O to Float16_b precision despite fp32_dest_acc_en. THE PV MATMUL OUTPUT (cb_pv) is fp32-exact but Phase 10 SFPU O=alpha*O+PV writes fp16-precision O to cb_o_acc. Baseline 9% flip source = O stored at fp16 precision.

Host model: O truncated to IEEE-half (Float16_b) reproduces device O0 EXACTLY (95.25/96.1875/96.5625/94.125) and device output EXACTLY. cb_o_acc holds O at fp16 precision not fp32. Likely UnpackToDestMode not configured so CopyTile of fp32 CBs into DEST truncates to fp16. Fix: enable UnpackToDestFp32 for fp32 CBs (cb_pv, cb_o_acc, etc) so copy_tile is fp32-exact. This is the dominant baseline 9% flip source.
